concerns.text: Plan fits polynomial regression with squared terms (crossing_number², braid_index²) on discrete integer predictors. With small integer values (c≤13, b≤c), polynomial terms have limited interpretability and may overfit. Consider justification for polynomial form or alternative non-linear specifications (e.g., splines, piecewise).

Filtering torus/satellite knots (volume=0/undefined) creates selection bias. Analysis only generalizes to hyperbolic knots, not all prime knots. This limitation should be explicitly stated in final reports as affecting generalizability, not just noted in docs/excluded_knots.md.

Plan computes arc index, Seifert circle count, bridge number with known mathematical constraints (e.g., bridge ≤ crossing). Plan should verify these inequalities empirically hold for the dataset before using these invariants in analysis, per Constitution Principle VI (Mathematical Invariant Consistency).

Sample size not addressed: ~632 prime knots at c≤10, but many are non-hyperbolic. After filtering for hyperbolic volume, sample may be underpowered for regression with multiple predictors. Plan should include power analysis or minimum sample size justification for regression models.
